name: Angel of the Winds Casino
notes: Tulalip-area casino. Concerts, dining, hotel.

name: Edmonds Center for the Arts
notes: Performing arts venue. Music, theater, comedy, community events.

name: Edmonds City Hall
notes: City government, permits, council.

name: Salish Sea Brewing
notes: Craft brewery in downtown Edmonds. Near ferry terminal.

name: Edmonds Fishing Pier
notes: Year-round fishing. Squid jigging at night in summer. Adjacent to underwater dive park.

name: Boeing Credit Union
notes: BECU branch. Originally Boeing Employees Credit Union. Across from Tetos.

name: Pattys Eggnest Cafe
notes: Breakfast and brunch. Near 112th and Evergreen Way corridor.

name: Tetos 112th Street Cafe
notes: Classic diner. Breakfast all day, banana bread French toast, Eggs Benedict. Across from Boeing Credit Union. Generous portions, reasonable prices. Local favorite.

name: Boeing Everett Factory
notes: Worlds largest building by volume. 787, 777X, 767 assembly. Public tours.

name: Flying Heritage & Combat Armor Museum
notes: Paul Allen collection. WWII aircraft and tanks. Weekend flights of vintage aircraft.

name: Future of Flight
notes: Boeing tour departure. Aviation exhibits, rooftop viewing platform.

name: Museum of Flight Restoration Center
notes: Watch aircraft restoration in progress. Free admission.

name: Paine Field Terminal
notes: Alaska Airlines commercial flights. Opened 2019. Boutique terminal.

name: Everett City Hall
notes: City government, permits, council. Phone: (425) 257-8700.

name: Everett Police Department
notes: Non-emergency: (425) 257-8450. Emergency: 911.

name: Schack Art Center
notes: Community art center. Glassblowing, galleries, workshops.

name: Village Theatre
notes: Professional musical theatre. Issaquah + Everett campuses.

name: Casino Road food corridor
notes: Dozens of restaurants: Vietnamese pho, Korean BBQ, Mexican taquerias, Somali, Filipino, Indian. Best affordable diverse food in south Everett.

name: Everett Civic Auditorium
notes: Concert and event venue. Capacity ~1,700.

name: Xfinity Arena (Angel of the Winds Arena)
notes: Everett Silvertips (WHL hockey). Concerts. Capacity ~8,100.

name: Imagine Children's Museum
notes: Interactive children's museum. 3 floors of hands-on exhibits.

name: Funko HQ
notes: Funko headquarters. Store with exclusives, history, photo ops.

name: Jetty Island
notes: Free summer ferry (Jul-Sep). 2-mile sandy beach, warm shallow water, bird watching.

name: Port of Everett Marina
notes: 13-lane boat launch ($15/day). Direct access to Possession Sound. Crabbing, fishing, shrimping.

name: Scuttlebutt Brewing
notes: Waterfront brewpub at the marina. Local craft beer since 1996.

name: Snohomish County SCOPI
notes: Interactive parcel map. Ownership, zoning, land use data. Essential for park proposal research.

name: H Mart
notes: Korean grocery. Produce, seafood, prepared foods, food court.

name: Alderwood Mall
notes: Major regional shopping center. Dining, retail, entertainment.

name: Alderwood Mall
notes: Regional mall. Nordstrom, Macys, JCPenney. Dining, entertainment. Near I-5 and 196th.

name: Best Buy
notes: Electronics retail.

name: Dicks Sporting Goods
notes: Sporting goods. Fishing, camping, outdoor gear.

name: Five Guys
notes: Burgers and fries.

name: Lynnwood City Center Station
notes: Sound Transit 1 Line and 2 Line terminus. Bus connections to Mukilteo, Everett, Edmonds.

name: Lynnwood City Hall
notes: City government. Adjacent to new City Center light rail station.

name: Olive Garden
notes: Italian-American dining. Near Alderwood Mall.

name: Target
notes: Department store. Near the mall.

name: AutoZone
notes: Auto parts.

name: Costco
notes: Warehouse club. Gas, groceries, bulk goods.

name: Home Depot
notes: Home improvement. Building materials, tools, garden. Near I-5/196th.

name: Lowes
notes: Home improvement competitor. Near Highway 99 corridor.

name: O'Reilly Auto Parts
notes: Auto parts.

name: Panda Express
notes: Quick service Chinese.

name: Winco Foods
notes: Employee-owned bulk grocery. Low prices.

name: Kavanagh Park
notes: Neighborhood park near Paine Field. Playground, sports field.

name: Eye Candy Cookie
notes: Specialty cookies and sweets. Made to order.

name: Japanese Gulch
notes: 160 acres, 8+ mi trail. Urban forest preserve. History: 1903 Japanese lumber community.

name: 92nd Street Park
notes: Neighborhood park with playground and open space.

name: Safeway
notes: Full grocery. Beverly Park area between Mukilteo and Lynnwood.

name: Elliot Point Park
notes: Waterfront park near the old ferry terminal site.

name: Lighthouse Park Fishing Pier
notes: New public fishing pier. Coho, Pink salmon in season.

name: Mukilteo Lighthouse Park
notes: Historic lighthouse, beach, boat launch, picnic. THE Mukilteo park.

name: A Burger Place
notes: Casual burgers in the shopping center.

name: Bits Craft Cocktails
notes: Craft cocktails, rotating wine, draft beer, small plates. Wed-Sat evenings.

name: Harbour Pointe Park
notes: Neighborhood park in Harbour Pointe. Playground, picnic area.

name: Mod Pizza
notes: Build-your-own artisan pizza. Fast casual.

name: Mud Bay
notes: PNW pet supply chain. Natural food, treats, supplies.

name: Pointe Restaurant & Lounge
notes: Restaurant at the golf club. Open to public.

name: Providence Medical Center Mukilteo
notes: Urgent care and medical offices.

name: QFC Harbour Pointe
notes: Full grocery + gas station. Anchor tenant.

name: Red Cork Bistro
notes: Approachable fine dining. Washington wines, craft cocktails. Harbour Pointe Shopping Center.

name: Rite Aid
notes: Pharmacy.

name: Teriyaki Miako
notes: Chinese and teriyaki. Popular local spot.

name: Thai Rama
notes: Thai restaurant in Harbour Village strip.

name: Umami Asian Cuisine
notes: Asian fusion. Harbour Village.

name: Mukilteo City Council
notes: City council adopted Japanese Gulch Master Plan 2016. Target for park petition.

name: Mukilteo City Hall
notes: City government. Council meetings, permits, planning. Phone: (425) 263-8000.

name: Mukilteo Library
notes: Sno-Isle Libraries system. Community meeting rooms. Free wifi.

name: Mukilteo Police Department
notes: Non-emergency: (425) 263-8100. Emergency: 911.

name: Azteca Mexican
notes: Mexican restaurant on the Speedway near the Harbour Pointe intersection.

name: Diamond Knot Brewing
notes: Local craft brewery. Burgers, fish & chips, house beers. Established 1994.

name: Harbour Pointe Golf Club
notes: 18-hole championship course. Restaurant. South Mukilteo.

name: Ivars Mukilteo Landing
notes: Waterfront seafood. Clam chowder, fish & chips. Views of ferry traffic and Whidbey Island.

name: Japanese Gulch
notes: 160-acre urban forest. Historic Japanese logging community site. Trail network.

name: Mukilteo Community YMCA
notes: Pool, gym, classes, childcare. Youth sports programs.

name: Mukilteo Lighthouse
notes: Historic 1906 lighthouse. Beach, boat launch, picnic area. Free admission.

name: Red Cup Cafe
notes: Breakfast and brunch. Popular weekends. Local favorite.

name: Walgreens
notes: Pharmacy and convenience. On the Speedway.

name: Whidbey Coffee
notes: Drive-through and walk-in espresso. Local roaster from Whidbey Island.

name: Blu Burgers
notes: Burger spot at the south end of the proposed park site. Visible in satellite view.

name: Holiday Inn Express Mukilteo
address: Near Harbour Pl & Paine Field Blvd
notes: Adjacent to the proposed park greenspace. West side of 525 interchange.

name: Proposed Paine Field Community Park
address: Between Paine Field Blvd and W Perimeter Rd
notes: Potential community park on ~40 acres of undeveloped forest and cleared land. Adjacent to Boeing perimeter. Old go-kart/track loop visible on east side. Brown cleared field on west side near 99th Pl. Following Japanese Gulch preservation model. Parcel research needed via SCOPI.

name: Fowler Park
notes: Community park. Tennis courts, playground, picnic shelters.

name: Traxx Indoor Raceway
notes: Indoor go-kart facility opened Dec 1998. 32 karts, quarter-mile track, 17 turns. Operated 24 years by Jan and Rick Wanzer. One of the first indoor tracks in the country.

name: Mukilteo Boys & Girls Club
notes: After-school programs, summer camp, youth activities.

name: Mukilteo Community Garden
notes: Community garden plots available to residents.

name: Mukilteo Fire District
notes: Snohomish County Fire District 7. Stations in Mukilteo and Harbour Pointe.

name: KEXP
notes: Independent music station. Live sessions, DJ shows. The heart of PNW music. 90.3 FM.

name: Forterra
notes: Land conservation nonprofit. Helped save Japanese Gulch. Potential partner for Paine Field park effort.

name: Washington Trails Association
notes: Trail advocacy. Documents Japanese Gulch trails. Potential support for park trail network.

name: KNKX
notes: NPR + jazz/blues. Puget Sound public radio. Formerly KPLU. 88.5 FM.

name: Tulalip Resort Casino
notes: Full resort. Concert venue, spa, dining. Seattle Premium Outlets adjacent.
